Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: A comparative analysis of Inconel 718 made by additive manufacturing and suction casting: Microstructure evolution in homogenization

Process_Text.original: Suction-casting under pure argon atmosphere using an arc-melter (ABJ-338, Materials Research Furnaces Inc); rod shape sample with diameter of 11 mm and length of 40 mm.
Process_Text.simplified: Suction-casting using arc-melter (ABJ-338) under argon atmosphere

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxial grains formed during solidification with diameter between 10 and 20 μm. Irregular-shaped Nb-rich Laves_C14 phase forms along grain boundaries as the predominant precipitate. A small fraction of blocky NbC carbides is encompassed by the Laves_C14 precipitates. The formation temperature of NbC carbides (1298°C) is higher than that of the Laves_C14 phase (1170°C). δ phase not observed.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed grains (10-20 μm); Laves_C14 along grain boundaries; NbC carbides present; no δ phase

Properties_Info.Note: Total phase fraction of Nb-rich phases (NbC + Laves_C14)

Process_Text.original: Suction-casting under pure argon atmosphere using an arc-melter (ABJ-338); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 20 minutes with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon.
Process_Text.simplified: Suction-casting (ABJ-338 arc-melter) + homogenization at 1180°C for 20 min, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: 20-min homogenization at 1180°C dissolves most of the Laves_C14 phase in the as-cast microstructure and remains the NbC carbides formed during solidification. Some remaining Laves_C14 particles can still be observed near the NbC carbides. Unimodal grain size distribution indicating uniform grain growth. GOS increases compared to as-cast state due to strains introduced by grain growth and dissolution processes of Laves_C14 phase.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Most Laves_C14 dissolved; NbC carbides remain; unimodal grain size distribution

Properties_Info.Note: Total phase fraction of Nb-rich phases after 20 min homogenization; significant decrease from 13.13% to 0.73%

Process_Text.original: Suction-casting under pure argon atmosphere using an arc-melter (ABJ-338); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 1 hour with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon. EBSD mapping area 2500 μm × 2500 μm for large grain analysis.
Process_Text.simplified: Suction-casting (ABJ-338 arc-melter) + homogenization at 1180°C for 1 h, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: Some remaining Laves_C14 particles still observable near NbC carbides. Bimodal grain size distribution indicating discontinuous grain growth. Left peak of grain size distribution curve represents initial grains, right peak represents grown grains. GOS slightly decreases compared to AC20m. Abnormal grain growth initiates as indicated by the continuous decrease of z = 3f/4r factor representing dispersion level of secondary phase particles.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal grain size distribution; discontinuous grain growth; abnormal grain growth initiates

Properties_Info.Note: Total phase fraction of Nb-rich phases after 1 h homogenization

Process_Text.original: Suction-casting under pure argon atmosphere using an arc-melter (ABJ-338); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 12 hours with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon. EBSD mapping area 2500 μm × 2500 μm for large grain analysis.
Process_Text.simplified: Suction-casting (ABJ-338 arc-melter) + homogenization at 1180°C for 12 h, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: Laves_C14 phase dissolves completely; only NbC carbides remain as precipitates. Significantly coarsened grains with some small grains remaining at triple junctions of coarsened grains. Trimodal grain size distribution with two peaks appearing at smaller grain diameters of 150 μm and 400 μm. Abnormal grain growth attributed to continuous dissolution of Laves_C14 phase and decrease of dispersion level z = 3f/4r. GOS increases due to increased grain size from abnormal grain growth.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Laves_C14 completely dissolved; only NbC carbides remain; abnormal grain growth with trimodal distribution (peaks at 150 and 400 μm)

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Trimodal_Grain_Size_Distribution: Two peaks at 150 μm and 400 μm

Properties_Info.Note: Total phase fraction approaches equilibrium NbC value predicted by CALPHAD; consistent with 0.47% equilibrium NbC fraction

Process_Text.original: Built by LPBF using EOS M 290 machine with default laser melting parameters: laser power 285 W, scan velocity 960 mm/s, hatching space 0.11 mm. Hatch lines between adjacent layers have rotation angle of 67°. Samples in as-built state.
Process_Text.simplified: AM-LPBF (EOS M 290); laser power 285 W, scan speed 960 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.11 mm, layer rotation 67°

Microstructure_Text.original: Columnar grains grow along the direction normal to the edge of single arc-shaped melt pools due to large directional thermal gradient from laser melting. Both very fine columnar subgrains (width ~1 μm) and cellular subgrains (diameter ~2 μm) are found within the relatively larger grains of overlapped melt pool areas. The only secondary phase observed is Laves_C14 phase, which forms along grain boundaries of both columnar and cellular subgrains due to Nb segregation. No NbC carbide observed in as-built sample (probably suppressed by fast solidification rate). δ phase not observed.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar grains along BD; fine columnar subgrains (~1 μm) and cellular subgrains (~2 μm); Laves_C14 along subgrain boundaries; no NbC in as-built state

Properties_Info.Note: Total phase fraction of Nb-rich phases (Laves_C14) in as-built state; no NbC carbide observed

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: Built by LPBF using EOS M 290 machine with default laser melting parameters (laser power 285 W, scan velocity 960 mm/s, hatching space 0.11 mm, layer rotation 67°); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 20 minutes with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon.
Process_Text.simplified: AM-LPBF (EOS M 290) + homogenization at 1180°C for 20 min, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: Laves_C14 phase formed in as-built condition dissolves completely into gamma matrix within 20 min. Only a very small amount of NbC carbides form along grain boundaries. Small peak at grain diameter of 50 μm in grain size distribution indicates initiation of recrystallization. Relatively high GOS values due to distortion accumulation from residual stress introduced during LPBF process.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Laves_C14 completely dissolved; very small amount of NbC forming; recrystallization initiates; high GOS from LPBF residual stress

Properties_Info.Note: Nb-rich phases nearly absent after 20 min homogenization; Laves_C14 completely dissolved

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: Built by LPBF using EOS M 290 machine with default laser melting parameters (laser power 285 W, scan velocity 960 mm/s, hatching space 0.11 mm, layer rotation 67°); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 1 hour with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon.
Process_Text.simplified: AM-LPBF (EOS M 290) + homogenization at 1180°C for 1 h, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: Three distinct peaks observed in grain size distribution curve. Average grain size decreases to 128 μm from ~130 μm initial. NbC carbide precipitation continues with homogenization time. Almost all grains continuously recrystallized as indicated by lowest and uniform GOS distribution. More NbC carbides form along grain boundaries.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Three-peak grain size distribution; grain refinement to 128 μm; continuous recrystallization; NbC precipitation continues

Properties_Info.Note: Grain size decreases from ~130 μm initial to 128 μm after 1 h homogenization
NbC carbide fraction increasing from ~0% after 20 min to 0.05% after 1 h

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: Built by LPBF using EOS M 290 machine with default laser melting parameters (laser power 285 W, scan velocity 960 mm/s, hatching space 0.11 mm, layer rotation 67°); followed by homogenization at 1180°C for 12 hours with water quench (ice-water). Samples encapsulated in vacuumed quartz tubes with back-filled pure argon.
Process_Text.simplified: AM-LPBF (EOS M 290) + homogenization at 1180°C for 12 h, water quench

Microstructure_Text.original: Notable increase of NbC carbide particle size compared to AM20m and AM1h. Grain size distribution close to unimodal. Average grain size decreases further to 113 μm. Grain size not significantly increased compared to AM1h due to Zener pinning effects from NbC particles. Dispersed NbC particles distribute along and near grain boundaries, impeding grain boundary movement effectively. Large NbC particles (~200-300 nm) lose coherency with gamma matrix and act as dislocation sources. Small coherent NbC particles have orientation relationship: (0-22)_NbC[-233]_NbC/(1-1-1)_gamma[-1-12]_gamma. Planar dislocation arrays/loops present; dislocation tanglings and cells rarely found due to intermediate stacking fault energy (50-70 mJ/m²) of Inconel 718. Both Orowan bowing and dislocation cutting mechanisms observed. Dislocation pileup at grain boundaries activates new dislocations in adjacent grains.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Unimodal grain size (113 μm); significant NbC coarsening; Zener pinning by NbC prevents grain growth; dislocation arrays from incoherent NbC; Orowan bowing and cutting mechanisms

Properties_Info.Note: Grain size refinement maintained; further reduced from 128 μm at 1 h to 113 μm at 12 h
NbC fraction increases significantly from 0.05% at 1 h to 0.36% at 12 h; approaches equilibrium value
